title: JavaScript Placement Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with placed and external JS.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript?
options: Using External .js files linked via the <script src=''> tag at the END of the <body>.

questions.question: Which 'Pillar of the Web' is responsible for providing behavior and interactivity to a page (e.g., placing JS in the <head> or <body>)?
The 'defer' attribute on a <script> tag allows the browser to ________ while still downloading the script in the background.
options: Continue parsing the HTML page without interruption (giving the user a faster view).
Both 'Continue parsing' and 'Stay exactly the same' are correct.

questions.question: One of the most powerful use cases for 'Placement' is ________ (keeping your HTML clean and allowing for caching).
What is the primary difference between 'async' and 'defer' in a script tag?
options: 'async' executes as soon as it downloads, potentially interrupting the page load, while 'defer' waits until the entire HTML is finished parsing.
'defer' executes as soon as it downloads.
